ResourceHeader.Description: SHARP, the Solar Hard X-ray Polarimeter, is the hard X-ray polarimetry instrument on the PADRE, Solar Polarization and Directivity X-Ray Experiment, 12U solar-spinning CubeSat mission. SHARP is designed to measure the polarization of non-thermal hard X-rays emitted during the impulsive phase of solar flares. The instrument measures the azimuthal distribution of Compton-scattered photons using a cylindrical beryllium scatterer surrounded by cadmium telluride, CdTe, photon-counting detectors instrumented with Timepix3 application-specific integrated circuits. These observations are used to constrain the angular distribution of flare-accelerated electrons and to help distinguish strongly beamed from more isotropic particle-acceleration scenarios.

InformationURL.Name: PADRE Instrument Web Page
InformationURL.Description: PADRE instrument overview page describing SHARP and MeDDEA.

Instrument.InvestigationName: Solar Hard X-ray Polarimeter (SHARP) on PADRE

OperatingSpan.Note: StartDate is the PADRE launch date.
